file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16-AMENDMENT TO THE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT BETWEEN INGRAM MICRO AND NETGEAR.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be voidable.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, with such permission at the assigning party's sole discretion. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be automatically valid.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states assignment requires permission, which cannot be unreasonably withheld or delayed, thus implying a process where permission could be denied. The modified text eliminates the need for seeking external approval for the agreement and asserts it is at the assigning party's discretion, and any attempt to assign is automatically valid. This directly contradicts the original provision and creates uncertainty regarding the assignment process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, with such permission at the assigning party's sole discretion. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be automatically valid.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It first states that assignments can be made without prior written permission but then says that any attempt to assign without such permission will be automatically valid. The contradiction arises from the statement that permission is not needed and that any attempt to assign without permission will be considered valid. The original text states assignment requires permission, which cannot be unreasonably withheld or delayed. This change causes a contradiction to the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall supplement any terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement are superseded by this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the amendment supersedes any conflicting terms and all other terms remain unchanged. The modified text contradicts this by saying the amendment supplements the original agreement and all other terms are superseded, creating complete uncertainty about what terms are actually active or superseded.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall supplement any terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement are superseded by this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally alters the relationship between the amendment and the original agreement, creating a direct contradiction. The original text states that the amendment supersedes conflicting terms but leaves other terms unchanged. The changed text states that the amendment supplements the original agreement and supersedes all other terms, creating an in-text contradiction within the document as described under the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.
